walking_skeleton_strategy: C-local — real SQLAlchemy + in-memory aiosqlite + boto3.Stubber-wrapped MinIOLakeRepository (no compose stack required; the substrate is exactly what backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py already drives the existing 15 tests against — Iron Rule binds the refactor to that same substrate)
iron_rule_fence: backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py — all 15 existing tests stay byte-for-byte green across every phase. Zero assertion edits, zero fixture-shape edits. The acceptance suite is PARALLEL to these characterization tests, NOT a replacement.

Per ADR-022 §Confirmation, after DELIVER all 15 tests pass byte-for-byte AND one new absence-assertion test (DWD-2) is added at the end of the existing test file — that single addition is the only mutation to test_create_dataset_from_upload.py allowed across the entire feature.

phases.title: Phase 00 — Walking skeleton green: dispatcher exists + canonicalization in place + use case still returns external shapes
phases.scope: ADR-022's Mikado-step 0+1+2+3+4 collapsed into a single shippable phase that takes the walking skeleton green. Implement: (a) backend/app/use_cases/dataset/_pipeline/plugin_dispatch.py — NEW module containing UploadPluginDispatcher class (~80 LOC). Public method dispatch(event, raw_content, choices) -> MultiProcessingResult. Encapsulates plugin lookup precedence (get_by_name then get_for_filename; verbatim from current use-case lines 79-104), asyncio.to_thread + 120s timeout, _converted_content side-channel persistence (HL7v2->FHIR), no-registry CSV fallback. Always wraps single-result plugins as MultiProcessingResult with len(results)==1 — the canonicalization (DWD-1 in DESIGN's wave-decisions.md). (b) backend/app/plugins/protocol.py — relax MultiProcessingResult.__post_init__: r.name is None check applies only when len(self.results) > 1 (DWD-3 in DESIGN). One-line conditional. (c) backend/app/use_cases/dataset/create_dataset_from_upload.py — refactor body to delegate to UploadPluginDispatcher; replace isinstance(result, MultiProcessingResult) branch with a single linear loop over canonical.results; ADD the explicit `if len(results) > 1:` guard for outbox payload (DWD-2 HARD GATE — must appear in code, not as comment, not as natural outcome). External signature unchanged: Result[Dataset | list[Dataset], str]; len-1 list unwrapped to Dataset before return. (d) Wire the acceptance suite's repository_container + db_engine fixtures in tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/conftest.py — replace the pytest.skip stubs with real engine + container construction (mirroring backend/tests/conftest.py's db_session fixture). Replace pytest.fail bodies in steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py for the walking-skeleton scenario only (Background steps + given_project_exists + given_upload_event + given_stub_returns_csv + when_run_use_case + the WS-specific @then steps). (e) Confirm Iron-Rule fence: all 15 existing tests at backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py stay byte-for-byte green.
scenarios_to_unskip: tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/walking-skeleton.feature :: Customer uploads a single CSV and receives a single dataset back through the new dispatcher
phases.files_changed_estimate: backend/app/use_cases/dataset/_pipeline/plugin_dispatch.py — NEW, ~80 LOC. backend/app/plugins/protocol.py — EDIT, +3 LOC (one-line validator relaxation + a defensive comment). backend/app/use_cases/dataset/create_dataset_from_upload.py — REWRITE body, ~64 LOC removed and ~30 LOC added (use case shrinks from ~174 LOC to ~110 LOC; external signature unchanged). backend/app/use_cases/dataset/_pipeline/__init__.py — EDIT, +1 LOC (re-export UploadPluginDispatcher if needed; DELIVER decides at the import site per upstream-changes.md). tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/conftest.py — REWRITE, replace pytest.skip stubs with real engine+container fixtures, ~80 LOC. tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py — EDIT, replace ~12 pytest.fail step bodies with real implementations (walking-skeleton steps only; milestone-1 + milestone-2 + milestone-3 step bodies stay scaffolded behind @pending).
exit_criteria: Walking-skeleton scenario green end-to-end: `cd tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity && uv run --no-project pytest -m 'walking_skeleton'`

All 15 existing tests at backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py green byte-for-byte (`cd backend && uv run pytest tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py`). Iron Rule binding — `git diff backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py` MUST show zero modifications.
Whole backend test suite green: `cd backend && uv run pytest`
`grep -n 'isinstance(result, MultiProcessingResult)' backend/app/use_cases/dataset/create_dataset_from_upload.py` returns zero matches
`grep -nE 'PluginRegistry|FileFormatPlugin|csv_parser' backend/app/use_cases/dataset/create_dataset_from_upload.py` returns zero matches
`grep -n 'if len(results) > 1' backend/app/use_cases/dataset/create_dataset_from_upload.py` returns exactly one match (DWD-2 binding effect on DELIVER: the guard MUST appear explicitly in code)
MultiProcessingResult([ProcessingResult(df=df)]) (single, no name) constructs without raising (DWD-3 binding: validator relaxed for len==1)
MultiProcessingResult([ProcessingResult(df=df1), ProcessingResult(df=df2)]) with both names==None still raises ValueError (DWD-3 binding: safety property preserved for len>1)

Reviewer (nw-software-crafter-reviewer) approves: hexagonal boundary respected (steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py imports nothing from app.plugins or app.use_cases.dataset._pipeline.plugin_dispatch except where the import-graph proof step explicitly does — that is the dispatcher's existence assertion, NOT an internal-component invocation)
ac_references: DESIGN §2 Option α (recommended) + §4 Recommendation + §5 Migration order
DESIGN/wave-decisions.md DWD-1 (UploadPluginDispatcher class), DWD-3 (validator relaxation), DWD-5 (external return shape preserved), DWD-6 (Mikado order steps 0-4)

distill/wave-decisions.md DWD-1 (real-IO via SQLite + boto3.Stubber), DWD-2 (asymmetry-preservation HARD GATE — Phase 02 will pin both halves), DWD-4 (walking-skeleton scope = single-file CSV through dispatcher)

phases.title: Phase 01 — Milestone 1 dispatcher extraction scenarios green (single, multi, fallback, precedence)
phases.scope: Build out the dispatcher unit-test layer (~5 unit tests at backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_plugin_dispatch.py per ADR-022 §Confirmation: plugin lookup precedence in isolation; no-registry CSV fallback; _converted_content side-channel persistence; 120s timeout firing; single-result wrapping shape) AND turn on the four milestone-1 acceptance scenarios. The dispatcher exists in production after Phase 00; this phase pins its observable behaviour at both unit AND acceptance levels. Replace pytest.fail bodies in steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py for the milestone-1 step bindings (given_upload_event_with_plugin / given_upload_event_no_plugin, given_stub_returns_raw_bytes, given_registry_single / given_registry_recording_single / given_registry_extra_unknown_plugin / given_registry_multi / given_no_registry, then_canonical_one_entry / then_canonical_n_entries / then_canonical_one_entry_singular, then_recording_plugin_invoked / then_extension_plugin_not_invoked) with real implementations. Add a recording wrapper around UploadPluginDispatcher.dispatch in the test fixture so the dispatcher_canonical_lengths capture entry can be observed without coupling production code to test concerns (the wrapper lives in the conftest, not in the dispatcher class).
scenarios_to_unskip: tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-1-dispatcher-extraction.feature :: Single-plugin upload produces a one-entry canonical pipeline result
tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-1-dispatcher-extraction.feature :: Multi-plugin upload produces an N-entry canonical pipeline result
tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-1-dispatcher-extraction.feature :: No-registry CSV fallback still produces a one-entry canonical pipeline result
tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-1-dispatcher-extraction.feature :: Plugin name on the event takes precedence over filename extension matching
phases.files_changed_estimate: backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_plugin_dispatch.py — NEW, ~150 LOC (5 unit tests + small fixture set; mirrors test shape of test_create_dataset_from_upload.py for consistency). tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/conftest.py — EDIT, +20 LOC (add recording-wrapper fixture for dispatcher.dispatch). tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py — EDIT, replace ~14 pytest.fail step bodies with real implementations.
exit_criteria: All 4 milestone-1 acceptance scenarios green: `cd tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity && uv run --no-project pytest -m 'milestone_1'`
Phase 00 walking-skeleton scenario still green
All 5 dispatcher unit tests in backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_plugin_dispatch.py green: `cd backend && uv run pytest tests/use_cases/dataset/test_plugin_dispatch.py`
All 15 existing tests at backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py still green byte-for-byte
Whole backend test suite green: `cd backend && uv run pytest`
Recording wrapper around UploadPluginDispatcher.dispatch lives in the acceptance conftest, NOT in the production class (production class stays free of test concerns)
Reviewer approves: dispatcher unit tests cover the five concerns identified in ADR-022 §Confirmation (precedence, fallback, side-channel, timeout, single-result wrapping)

distill/wave-decisions.md DWD-5 (4 milestone-1 scenarios cover the full dispatcher contract)

phases.title: Phase 02 — Milestone 2 asymmetry-preservation scenarios green (CRITICAL HARD GATE)
phases.scope: ADR-022's Mikado step 5 — add the new absence-assertion characterization test pinning 'single-result path does NOT call outbox_repo.update_payload' (DWD-2 in DESIGN's wave-decisions.md). The absence-assertion test is added INSIDE backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py — appended to TestCreateDatasetFromUploadCharacterization as a new method test_single_dataset_does_not_persist_dataset_ids_or_dataset_id_in_outbox_payload. This is the ONLY allowed mutation to test_create_dataset_from_upload.py across the entire feature (Iron Rule fence in the feature top-level). Then turn on the three milestone-2 acceptance scenarios: single-path absence, multi-path presence, and the explicit boundary scenario that runs both paths in one scenario to assert the `len(results) > 1` guard observably toggles. Replace pytest.fail bodies in steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py for the milestone-2 step bindings (then_outbox_payload_lacks_key, then_outbox_one_entry_lacks_key, then_outbox_two_entry_has_key, then_outbox_dataset_ids_length, then_outbox_dataset_id_matches_first, when_run_use_case_twice, given_canonical_single, given_canonical_multi).
scenarios_to_unskip: tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-2-asymmetry-preservation.feature :: Single-file upload leaves the outbox payload free of dataset_ids and dataset_id keys
tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-2-asymmetry-preservation.feature :: Multi-file upload records dataset_ids and dataset_id in the outbox payload
tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-2-asymmetry-preservation.feature :: The boundary that determines the payload write is the canonical result length being greater than one
phases.files_changed_estimate: backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py — APPEND ONE METHOD, +~50 LOC (new test_single_dataset_does_not_persist_dataset_ids_or_dataset_id_in_outbox_payload appended to TestCreateDatasetFromUploadCharacterization). EXISTING 15 METHODS UNTOUCHED — Iron Rule fence. tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py — EDIT, replace ~8 pytest.fail step bodies with real implementations.
exit_criteria: All 3 milestone-2 acceptance scenarios green: `cd tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity && uv run --no-project pytest -m 'milestone_2'`
Phase 00 walking-skeleton + Phase 01 milestone-1 scenarios still green

New test_single_dataset_does_not_persist_dataset_ids_or_dataset_id_in_outbox_payload green: `cd backend && uv run pytest tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py::TestCreateDatasetFromUploadCharacterization::test_single_dataset_does_not_persist_dataset_ids_or_dataset_id_in_outbox_payload`
All 15 PRE-EXISTING tests at backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py still green byte-for-byte (verify by `git diff` showing only the appended method)
Whole backend test suite green: `cd backend && uv run pytest`

DWD-2 binding effect verified: a deliberate code-side mutation that drops the `if len(results) > 1` guard fails BOTH the new absence-assertion test AND the milestone-2 acceptance scenarios. Reviewer mutates locally, confirms RED, reverts.

Reviewer approves: the absence-assertion test uses the OutboxRecord's persisted payload (db.get(OutboxRecord, upload_id)) to assert key absence, NOT a mock-call assertion (Dim 7 — observable behaviour, not internal method-call counts)
ac_references: DESIGN/wave-decisions.md DWD-2 (HARD GATE — single-path absence-assertion characterization test required)
distill/wave-decisions.md DWD-2 (mirrors DESIGN DWD-2 + binds the acceptance-level pin)

phases.title: Phase 03 — Milestone 3 call-site stability scenarios green + ADR-022 promote to Accepted
phases.scope: ADR-022's Mikado step 6 — add the pytest-archon import-graph rule per DWD-7 in DESIGN's wave-decisions.md (`app.use_cases.dataset.create_dataset_from_upload` MUST NOT import `app.plugins.PluginRegistry`, `app.plugins.protocol.FileFormatPlugin`, or `app.utils.csv_parser`). Then turn on the four milestone-3 acceptance scenarios that pin controller envelope, status codes, and error shapes — these are characterization assertions about the existing `HTTPController.post_dataset` boundary (DESIGN §5: 'Single in-tree caller of the use case'). The multi-upload scenario specifically pins the latent TypeError as today's observable (ADR-022 follow-up #3 — out of scope to fix; in scope to characterize). Replace pytest.fail bodies in steps/upload_pipeline_steps.py for the milestone-3 step bindings (given_controller_bound, when_post_through_controller, then_controller_status, then_envelope_data_type, then_envelope_self_link, then_controller_multi_unchanged, then_controller_non_success, then_controller_domain_failure, then_controller_storage_failure). Promote ADR-022 status from Proposed to Accepted (Atlas's design-reviewer pass should already have completed; if not, BLOCK on Atlas approval).

tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-3-call-site-stability.feature :: When no plugin matches and the file is not valid CSV, the controller surfaces the same error shape
tests/acceptance/refactor-upload-pipeline-modularity/milestone-3-call-site-stability.feature :: Partial-failure multi-upload preserves today's Failure shape at the controller boundary

deliver_dispatch_notes.paradigm: Python OOP (per CLAUDE.md backend conventions). UploadPluginDispatcher is a per-call coordinator class — not registered with RepositoryContainer (DWD-8 in DESIGN's wave-decisions.md: use-case-internal coordinator, not a driven adapter, no probe). Dispatcher's __init__ takes (registry, lake_repo, outbox_repo, timeout=120.0). Dispatcher's dispatch method is async; uses asyncio.to_thread + asyncio.wait_for verbatim from the current use-case body. The use case constructs the dispatcher inline per call (DESIGN §5: `dispatcher = UploadPluginDispatcher(...); multi_result = await dispatcher.dispatch(...)`).
deliver_dispatch_notes.iron_rule: NEVER modify a failing acceptance test to make it pass. Each phase enables ONE scenario at a time per the Outside-In TDD outer loop. After 3 failed attempts, revert and escalate. CRITICAL FOR THIS REFACTOR: NEVER edit any of the 15 existing tests at backend/tests/use_cases/dataset/test_create_dataset_from_upload.py — those are the Iron-Rule fence. Phase 02 is the ONLY phase allowed to mutate that file, and the only allowed mutation is APPENDING the absence-assertion test method to TestCreateDatasetFromUploadCharacterization. If a pre-existing test fails after a refactor step, the refactor is wrong, not the test.
deliver_dispatch_notes.tdd_cycle: RED_ACCEPTANCE → unskip one scenario (remove @pending or run with -m 'walking_skeleton or milestone_1') → RED_UNIT (write failing dispatcher unit test in test_plugin_dispatch.py for Phase 01) → GREEN (minimum implementation) → REFACTOR per RPP L1-L6 → next scenario.
